If you are a landowner or property owner with a Vodafone telecommunications mast on your site, you are almost certainly dealing with one of the largest and most established operators in the United Kingdom. Vodafone has spent decades refining its approach to mast lease negotiations, and without proper specialist representation, landlords routinely find themselves accepting terms that fall well below fair market value. At The Phone Mast Advice Company, we negotiate Vodafone mast leases every single week. Our team of specialist telecoms surveyors has accumulated more than twenty-five years of direct experience acting exclusively on behalf of landlords — never operators — and we understand precisely how Vodafone structures its negotiations, what tactics its agents employ, and where the genuine leverage lies in any given lease situation.

Whether you are facing a Vodafone lease renewal, have been approached about a rent review, or have received correspondence from Cornerstone or Vantage Towers that you do not fully understand, we can help you secure the right outcome. We provide detailed rental advice, negotiate the contractual terms your lease requires, and work closely with specialist telecom solicitors to oversee the entire lease process through to completion.

Vodafone in the UK: A Twenty-Year Overview

Understanding who you are dealing with is the first step in any successful lease negotiation. Vodafone is not simply a mobile phone company — it is a sprawling multinational corporation with a layered infrastructure strategy that directly affects the lease sitting on your land. Here is how Vodafone’s UK mast estate has evolved over the past two decades, and why that history matters to you as a landlord.

Vodafone has been part of the British mobile landscape since the very earliest days of cellular telephony. As one of the original UK mobile operators, Vodafone built out an extensive network of macro cell sites across England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland throughout the 1990s and 2000s. By the early 2010s, however, the economics of maintaining thousands of individual tower sites began to shift. The industry was moving toward infrastructure sharing, and Vodafone was at the forefront of that transition.

In 2012, Vodafone and O2 formed a landmark joint venture called Cornerstone Telecommunications Infrastructure Limited, known as CTIL. This entity consolidated the management of more than 18,000 mobile sites across the UK, creating one of the largest shared infrastructure platforms in Europe. The rationale was straightforward: by pooling their tower assets, both operators could reduce capital expenditure while expanding network coverage. For landlords, however, this consolidation introduced a new layer of corporate complexity — suddenly, the party managing your lease might not be the operator whose equipment was on your roof or in your field.

The consolidation deepened in 2017 when Vodafone and O2 completed Project Beacon, a comprehensive mast-sharing programme that saw both operators systematically co-locate equipment across Cornerstone’s portfolio. Sites that had previously hosted a single operator’s apparatus were now carrying equipment for both Vodafone and O2, yet the lease terms — and critically, the rent — often remained unchanged.

In 2021, Vodafone transferred its fifty per cent stake in Cornerstone to Vantage Towers, a subsidiary it had established as a dedicated tower company and subsequently floated on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange. This move was part of a broader European strategy to monetise Vodafone’s tower infrastructure, and it added yet another corporate entity into the chain of relationships that landlords must navigate.

The most significant recent development came with the proposed merger between Vodafone UK and Three UK, owned by CK Hutchison. Announced in 2023 and scrutinised extensively by the Competition and Markets Authority, the merger received CMA approval in December 2024 and was formally completed in June 2025. The combined entity is now the United Kingdom’s largest mobile network by total subscriber numbers, with Vodafone holding a fifty-one per cent stake and CK Hutchison forty-nine per cent. For landlords, this merger raises immediate questions about the future of existing lease arrangements, rent levels, and whether site rationalisation could put certain mast locations at risk.

How Vodafone Manages Its Mast Estate

One of the most frequent sources of confusion we encounter when advising landlords is the question of who actually controls a Vodafone mast site. The answer is rarely straightforward, and it has become considerably more complicated in recent years.

Vodafone’s UK mast infrastructure is primarily managed through Cornerstone, the joint venture originally created with O2. Cornerstone oversees approximately 14,200 macro cell sites — the large towers and rooftop installations that form the backbone of Vodafone’s network — as well as around 1,400 smaller micro sites used to boost coverage in dense urban areas and indoor environments. The day-to-day management of these sites, including lease administration, site maintenance, and upgrade works, is handled by Cornerstone’s operational team.

However, since 2021, Vodafone’s economic interest in Cornerstone sits within Vantage Towers, a separately listed company with its own commercial objectives. Vantage Towers is incentivised to maximise tower revenues and minimise ground rents — objectives that can run directly counter to landlords’ interests. When you receive a letter about your lease renewal, it may come from Cornerstone, from Vantage Towers, from Vodafone directly, or even from a third-party surveying firm acting as agent. Each of these parties has slightly different commercial motivations, and understanding those motivations is essential to negotiating effectively.

The Vodafone-Three merger adds a further dimension. Three UK’s mast estate was managed by a separate infrastructure company, and the process of integrating these two networks will inevitably lead to site rationalisation — some sites will be upgraded, others may become redundant. If your Vodafone mast site is identified as a candidate for decommissioning, you need expert advice immediately to protect your rental income and your legal position.

Our Specialist Approach to Vodafone Leases

At The Phone Mast Advice Company, we take a methodical, evidence-based approach to every Vodafone lease instruction.

When you instruct us, we begin with a comprehensive review of your existing lease documentation. We examine the rental history, the lease structure, the identity of the contracting parties, and the specific terms governing rent review and renewal. We then prepare a detailed rental assessment based on comparable evidence drawn from our extensive database of telecoms lease transactions across the United Kingdom. This is not guesswork — it is rigorous valuation work grounded in real market data.

Once we have established the rental position, we enter negotiations with Vodafone (or whichever entity is acting on their behalf) to secure improved terms for you. We negotiate not only on rent but on all the contractual provisions that affect your long-term interests: the duration of the lease, break clauses, upgrade and sharing provisions, reinstatement obligations, and access arrangements. Throughout this process, we collaborate closely with specialist telecom solicitors who handle the legal drafting and ensure that the lease documentation properly reflects the commercial terms we have agreed.

We act exclusively for landlords. We have never represented an operator, and we never will. This means there is no conflict of interest, no divided loyalty, and no temptation to recommend a quick settlement that benefits the operator at your expense. Our only objective is to achieve the best possible outcome for you.

Common Challenges with Vodafone Lease Negotiations

Landlords dealing with Vodafone face a number of recurring challenges that require specialist knowledge to overcome. These are not theoretical problems — they are issues we encounter in our casework week after week.

The Electronic Communications Code

Since the revised Electronic Communications Code came into force in December 2017 as part of the Digital Economy Act, operators including Vodafone have used its provisions to argue for substantially reduced rents. The Code introduced a new valuation methodology that strips out the value attributable to the mast itself, focusing instead on the underlying land value. Vodafone and its agents have been particularly firm in citing Code valuations during renewal negotiations, often presenting figures that bear little resemblance to the rents landlords have historically received. Our role is to scrutinise these figures, challenge the assumptions behind them, and present robust counter-evidence that supports a fair rent.

Rent Reduction Tactics

It is now standard practice for Vodafone (typically through Cornerstone) to open lease renewal negotiations with a dramatically reduced rental offer — sometimes as low as ten or fifteen per cent of the existing rent. These initial offers are not genuine reflections of market value; they are negotiating positions designed to anchor the discussion at a low starting point. Landlords who accept these offers without professional advice leave significant sums of money on the table. We ensure that does not happen.

Corporate Entity Confusion

As described above, the web of companies involved in a typical Vodafone mast site can be bewildering. Landlords receive correspondence from Cornerstone, Vantage Towers, Vodafone Limited, and various third-party agents, often without any clear explanation of how these entities relate to one another or which one holds the actual lease. This confusion is not accidental — it serves the operator’s interests by making it harder for landlords to identify the correct counterparty and assert their rights. We cut through this complexity and deal directly with the party that matters.

What We Do for Vodafone Landlords

Our services for landlords with Vodafone mast leases are comprehensive and tailored to your specific circumstances. We handle:

  • Rent Reviews — Detailed market assessments and negotiation of revised rents, whether your review is based on open market value, RPI indexation, or another mechanism specified in your lease.
  • Lease Renewals — Full management of the renewal process, from initial rental assessment through to completion of the new lease, including negotiation of all key contractual terms.
  • New Lettings — Advice and negotiation support if Vodafone or Cornerstone approaches you about installing a new mast on your land, ensuring you secure appropriate rent and robust lease terms from the outset.
  • Mast Lease Sales — Guidance on the sale of your mast lease rental income to a third-party investor, including valuation advice and assessment of offers.
  • Removal and Redevelopment — If you need a Vodafone mast removed to facilitate a development or for any other reason, we advise on your options and negotiate with the operator on your behalf.
  • Lease Retrieval and Review — If you do not have a copy of your lease or are unsure what terms it contains, we can obtain the documentation and provide a clear explanation of your rights and obligations.

Frequently Asked Questions About Vodafone Mast Leases

What rent should I expect for a Vodafone mast on my property?

Rental levels for Vodafone mast sites vary considerably depending on the location, type of installation, alternative site availability, and whether the lease falls under the Electronic Communications Code or the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954. Ground-level masts in rural areas typically command different rents to rooftop installations in urban centres. As a general principle, we always advise landlords to obtain a professional rental assessment before engaging in any negotiation. The figures Vodafone or Cornerstone initially propose are almost invariably below what can be achieved with proper representation.

Who actually manages my Vodafone lease — Vodafone, Cornerstone, or Vantage Towers?

This depends on the specific history and structure of your site. In most cases, the day-to-day management of your lease is handled by Cornerstone, which acts as the operational manager for the majority of Vodafone’s UK macro sites. However, Vodafone’s economic interest in Cornerstone is held through Vantage Towers, and Vodafone Limited itself may still be the named tenant on your lease. Identifying the correct entity — and understanding how these corporate relationships affect your negotiating position — is one of the first things we do when you instruct us.

Can Vodafone reduce my mast rent under the Electronic Communications Code?

The revised Electronic Communications Code does provide a framework under which operators can seek reduced rents, and Vodafone has been among the most active in pursuing this. However, a Code-based valuation is not a foregone conclusion — the outcome depends on a range of factors including the terms of your existing lease, whether the Code actually applies to your agreement, and the quality of the comparable evidence presented. We have substantial experience in resisting unjustified rent reductions and securing outcomes that properly reflect the value of our clients’ sites.

What happens to my Vodafone lease after the Three merger?

The completion of the Vodafone-Three merger in June 2025 does not automatically alter the terms of your existing lease. Your lease remains a binding contract between you and the named tenant, regardless of changes in the operator’s corporate structure. However, the merger may have practical implications — for example, if the combined network undertakes site rationalisation, your site could be earmarked for upgrade, additional equipment, or potentially decommissioning. We monitor these developments closely and advise our clients on the implications for their specific sites as information becomes available.

How long does a Vodafone lease renewal typically take?

In our experience, Vodafone lease renewals can take anywhere from six months to well over two years, depending on the complexity of the site, the parties involved, and whether there are disputed issues that require formal determination. Cornerstone’s internal processes can be slow, and delays are common — particularly where multiple corporate entities need to approve terms. Having a specialist surveyor managing the process helps to maintain momentum and avoid unnecessary delays that work against the landlord’s interests.

Should I negotiate directly with Vodafone or use a specialist?

We would always recommend instructing a specialist telecoms surveyor before entering into any negotiation with Vodafone or its agents. The operators employ teams of professional surveyors and solicitors whose sole objective is to minimise the rent they pay and maximise the contractual flexibility they enjoy. Negotiating against these professionals without equivalent expertise puts you at a significant disadvantage. Our fees are structured to reflect the value we add — and in the overwhelming majority of cases, our involvement results in a materially better outcome than a landlord could achieve on their own.

Get Expert Help with Your Vodafone Mast Lease

If you have a Vodafone mast on your property and want to ensure you are receiving a fair rent on proper terms, we are here to help. The Phone Mast Advice Company offers a free initial lease check to assess your current position and identify any areas where your lease could be improved.

Telephone us today on 01691 791543 to speak with one of our specialist telecoms surveyors. Alternatively, you can request a free lease check online or visit our contact page to send us a message. We have offices in London, Bristol, and Oswestry, and we act for landlords with Vodafone mast sites throughout the United Kingdom.

Do not leave your Vodafone lease negotiation to chance. Instruct the specialists who deal with these matters every day and who act solely in your interest.

This Cookie Policy was last updated on 23 July 2025 and applies to citizens and legal permanent residents of the United Kingdom.

1. Introduction

Our website, https://phonemastadvice.co.uk (hereinafter: “the website”) uses cookies and other related technologies (for convenience all technologies are referred to as “cookies”). Cookies are also placed by third parties we have engaged. In the document below we inform you about the use of cookies on our website.

2. What are cookies?

A cookie is a small simple file that is sent along with pages of this website and stored by your browser on the hard drive of your computer or another device. The information stored therein may be returned to our servers or to the servers of the relevant third parties during a subsequent visit.

3. What are scripts?

A script is a piece of program code that is used to make our website function properly and interactively. This code is executed on our server or on your device.

4. What is a web beacon?

A web beacon (or a pixel tag) is a small, invisible piece of text or image on a website that is used to monitor traffic on a website. In order to do this, various data about you is stored using web beacons.

5. Cookies

5.1 Technical or functional cookies

Some cookies ensure that certain parts of the website work properly and that your user preferences remain known. By placing functional cookies, we make it easier for you to visit our website. This way, you do not need to repeatedly enter the same information when visiting our website and, for example, the items remain in your shopping cart until you have paid. We may place these cookies without your consent.

5.2 Statistics cookies

We use statistics cookies to optimize the website experience for our users. With these statistics cookies we get insights in the usage of our website. We ask your permission to place statistics cookies.

5.3 Marketing/Tracking cookies

Marketing/Tracking cookies are cookies or any other form of local storage, used to create user profiles to display advertising or to track the user on this website or across several websites for similar marketing purposes.

5.4 Social media

On our website, we have included content from TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, X (Formerly Twitter), LinkedIn and WhatsApp to promote web pages (e.g. “like”, “pin”) or share (e.g. “tweet”) on social networks like TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, X (Formerly Twitter), LinkedIn and WhatsApp. This content is embedded with code derived from TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, X (Formerly Twitter), LinkedIn and WhatsApp and places cookies. This content might store and process certain information for personalized advertising.

TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, X (Formerly Twitter), LinkedIn and WhatsApp are located in the United States.

7. Consent

When you visit our website for the first time, we will show you a pop-up with an explanation about cookies. As soon as you click on “Save preferences”, you consent to us using the categories of cookies and plug-ins you selected in the pop-up, as described in this Cookie Policy. You can disable the use of cookies via your browser, but please note that our website may no longer work properly.

7.1 Manage your consent settings

You have loaded the Cookie Policy without javascript support. On AMP, you can use the manage consent button on the bottom of the page.

8. Enabling/disabling and deleting cookies

You can use your internet browser to automatically or manually delete cookies. You can also specify that certain cookies may not be placed. Another option is to change the settings of your internet browser so that you receive a message each time a cookie is placed. For more information about these options, please refer to the instructions in the Help section of your browser.

Please note that our website may not work properly if all cookies are disabled. If you do delete the cookies in your browser, they will be placed again after your consent when you visit our website again.

9. Your rights with respect to personal data

You have the following rights with respect to your personal data:

  • You have the right to know why your personal data is needed, what will happen to it, and how long it will be retained for.
  • Right of access: You have the right to access your personal data that is known to us.
  • Right to rectification: you have the right to supplement, correct, have deleted or blocked your personal data whenever you wish.
  • If you give us your consent to process your data, you have the right to revoke that consent and to have your personal data deleted.
  • Right to transfer your data: you have the right to request all your personal data from the controller and transfer it in its entirety to another controller.
  • Right to object: you may object to the processing of your data. We comply with this, unless there are justified grounds for processing.

To exercise these rights, please contact us. Please refer to the contact details at the bottom of this Cookie Policy. If you have a complaint about how we handle your data, we would like to hear from you, but you also have the right to submit a complaint to the supervisory authority (the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO)).

For Jersey residents, please contact the Jersey Office of The Information Commissioner. Guernsey residents can contact the Office of the Data Protection Authority in Guernsey.

10. Contact details

For questions and/or comments about our Cookie Policy and this statement, please contact us by using the following contact details:

The Phone Mast Advice Company Ltd.
20 – 22 Wenlock Road, London, Greater London N1 7GU
United Kingdom
Website: https://phonemastadvice.co.uk
Email: info@ex.comphonemastadvice.co.uk
Phone number: *442038830605‬

This Cookie Policy was synchronised with cookiedatabase.org on 30 May 2025.

Last updated: 24 March 2026

The Phone Mast Advice Company Ltd (“we”, “us”, “our”) is committed to protecting the privacy and security of your personal data. We take our responsibilities under the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR) and the Data Protection Act 2018 seriously, and we have designed this policy to be transparent about how we collect, use, store, and protect your information.

This privacy policy applies to all personal data we process in connection with our Phone Mast advisory services, our website at phonemastadvice.co.uk, and any communication you have with us by phone, email, or through our online forms.

1. Who We Are

We are The Phone Mast Advice Company Ltd, specialist advisors to UK landlords on telecommunications lease matters including rent reviews, lease renewals, new lettings, mast sales, removal and redevelopment, and electricity costs recovery.

Data Controller: The Phone Mast Advice Company Ltd
Registered Address: 167–169 Great Portland Road, 5th Floor, London, W1W 5PF
Additional Offices:
The Bristol Office, 2nd Floor, 5 High Street, Westbury on Trym, Bristol, BS9 3BY
The Fort Offices, Artillery Business Park, Oswestry, SY11 4AD
Email: info@phonemastadvice.co.uk
Telephone: 01691 791543

For any questions about this policy or how we handle your personal data, please contact us using the details above.

2. What Information We Collect

Depending on how you interact with us, we may collect and process the following categories of personal data:

Information you provide to us directly:

  • Your name, email address, telephone number, and postal address
  • Property and land details relevant to your Phone Mast enquiry (site address, property type, ownership details)
  • Copies of existing or proposed telecommunications lease agreements and related correspondence
  • Current and historical rent information
  • Details of the telecommunications operator(s) involved
  • Any other information you choose to share with us when seeking our advice

Information collected automatically when you visit our website:

  • Your IP address (anonymised where possible)
  • Browser type and version
  • Pages visited, time spent on pages, and navigation paths
  • Referring website or search terms
  • Device type and operating system

We do not collect any special category data (such as health, ethnicity, or political opinions), nor do we knowingly collect data from children under the age of 16.

3. How We Collect Your Information

We collect personal data through the following channels:

  • Website enquiry forms – when you submit a contact form, free rent estimate request, or free lease check form on our website
  • Email – when you write to us at info@phonemastadvice.co.uk or reply to our correspondence
  • Telephone – when you call us or we call you in relation to your enquiry
  • Post – when you send us documents by post
  • Social media – if you contact us via LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, or any other social media platform
  • Referrals – if another professional or existing client refers you to us, we may receive your name and contact details with your knowledge

4. Why We Use Your Information and Our Lawful Basis

We only process your personal data when we have a lawful basis to do so. The table below sets out each purpose and the corresponding legal basis under UK GDPR:

We will never use your personal data for any purpose other than those stated above. If we ever need to process your data for a new purpose, we will inform you and, where required, seek your consent before doing so.

5. Who We Share Your Information With

We do not sell, rent, trade, or share your personal data with any third party for their own purposes. Your data is used solely for the purpose of providing our advisory services to you.

In order to operate our business and deliver our services, we use a limited number of trusted service providers who process data on our behalf and under our instruction. These data processors include:

  • Website hosting provider – for securely hosting our website and storing form submissions
  • Customer relationship management (CRM) system – for managing client enquiries and communications
  • Google Analytics – for anonymised website usage analysis (only when you consent via our cookie banner)
  • Email service provider – for sending and receiving correspondence

Each of these processors is contractually bound to process your data only on our instructions and in accordance with UK data protection law. We carry out due diligence on all processors to ensure they provide appropriate security guarantees.

We may also disclose your information where required to do so by law, by a court order, or by a regulatory authority.

6. International Data Transfers

Some of the service providers we use to operate our website and manage enquiries are based outside the United Kingdom. Where your data is transferred internationally, we ensure that appropriate safeguards are in place, including:

  • Transfers to countries with an adequacy decision from the UK Government
  • The UK–US Data Bridge (under the EU–US Data Privacy Framework) for transfers to certified US organisations
  • Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the ICO where no adequacy decision exists

If you would like further information about the safeguards we have in place, please contact us.

7. How We Keep Your Information Safe

We take the security of your data seriously and have implemented appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect it, including:

  • Secure, encrypted connections (SSL/TLS) across our website
  • Access controls so that only authorised team members can view client data
  • Secure storage of physical documents at our offices
  • Regular review of our security practices and data processing procedures
  • Contractual security obligations on all data processors we engage

While no method of transmission or storage is completely secure, we are committed to maintaining the highest practical standard of data protection.

8. How Long We Keep Your Information

We only retain your personal data for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes for which it was collected. Our standard retention periods are:

When your data is no longer required, it is securely deleted or anonymised so that it can no longer be linked to you.

9. Cookies and Website Tracking

Our website uses cookies – small text files placed on your device – to help us improve your experience and understand how visitors use our site.

When you first visit our website, you will see a cookie consent banner that allows you to accept or reject non-essential cookies. We only place analytics and tracking cookies on your device if you give your consent.

Essential cookies are necessary for the website to function (for example, remembering your cookie preferences) and do not require consent.

Analytics cookies (such as Google Analytics) help us understand how visitors interact with our website so we can improve our content and services. These are only activated with your consent.

You can change your cookie preferences at any time using the cookie settings on our website. For more information, please see our Cookie Policy.

10. Your Rights Under UK GDPR

Under the UK GDPR, you have the following rights in relation to your personal data. You can exercise any of these rights free of charge by contacting us at info@phonemastadvice.co.uk.

We aim to respond to all legitimate requests within one calendar month. In exceptional circumstances, we may extend this by a further two months, but we will inform you if this is the case and explain why.

11. Social Media

We maintain a presence on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, and X (formerly Twitter). If you contact us through these platforms, we may receive personal data such as your name, username, and the content of your message.

Please be aware that interactions on social media platforms are also governed by each platform’s own privacy policy. We encourage you to review their policies before engaging with us through those channels.

12. Links to Other Websites

Our website may contain links to external websites. We are not responsible for the privacy practices or content of those sites. We encourage you to read the privacy policy of every website you visit.

13. Changes to This Privacy Policy

We keep this privacy policy under regular review to ensure it remains accurate and compliant with current legislation. Any changes will be published on this page with an updated “Last updated” date. We recommend checking this page periodically.

Where changes are significant, we will make reasonable efforts to notify you directly.

14. How to Contact Us or Make a Complaint

If you have any questions about this privacy policy, wish to exercise any of your rights, or have a concern about how we handle your data, please contact us:

The Phone Mast Advice Company Ltd
167–169 Great Portland Road, 5th Floor, London, W1W 5PF
Email: info@phonemastadvice.co.uk
Telephone: 01691 791543

If you are not satisfied with our response, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner’s Office (ICO), the UK’s independent supervisory authority for data protection:

Information Commissioner’s Office
Wycliffe House, Water Lane, Wilmslow, Cheshire, SK9 5AF
Website: ico.org.uk
Helpline: 0303 123 1113

We would always appreciate the opportunity to address your concerns directly before you approach the ICO, so please do contact us first.

While we strive to provide accurate and up-to-date information, we make no representations or warranties of any kind, express or implied, about the completeness, accuracy, reliability, suitability, or availability of the website or the information, products, services, or related graphics contained on the website for any purpose. 

The information provided on this website does not constitute legal, financial, or professional advice. It is important for users to exercise their own judgement and seek the advice of qualified professionals for any legal, financial, or professional matters.

Mentions of third-party products, services, or websites on our platform are for informational purposes only and do not imply endorsement or recommendation. The Phone Mast Advice Company Ltd is not associated with any of the mentioned operators, and we do not own the copyright to any logos or information pertaining to those operators on our website.

The website may contain links to external websites for additional information. However, we do not endorse or take responsibility for the content, accuracy, or availability of these external sites.

Users are responsible for their use of the website and should take necessary precautions to protect their devices from viruses or other harmful elements. The Phone Mast Advice Company Ltd shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages or losses arising from the use of or reliance on information provided on the website.

We provide recommendations based on industry knowledge, and users are under no obligation to use our services. The Phone Mast Advice Company Ltd is not engaged in competition between operators, and our goal is to offer impartial guidance to help users make informed decisions.

The website owner reserves the right to modify or update this disclaimer at any time without prior notice. Users are encouraged to review this disclaimer regularly for any changes.

[et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ fullwidth=”on” admin_label=”Hero Section” _builder_version=”4.16″ global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_fullwidth_header title=”Electricity Costs Recovery for Phone Mast Sites” button_one_text=”Enquire Now” button_one_url=”@ET-DC@eyJkeW5hbWljIjp0cnVlLCJjb250ZW50IjoicG9zdF9saW5rX3VybF9wYWdlIiwic2V0dGluZ3MiOnsicG9zdF9pZCI6IjYxIn19@” _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _dynamic_attributes=”button_one_url” title_font_size_phone=”1.6em” button_one_text_color_hover=”#edbb5f” button_two_text_color_hover=”#edbb5f” button_one_bg_color_hover=”rgba(0,0,0,0)” button_two_bg_color_hover=”rgba(0,0,0,0)”]

Recover Unpaid Electricity Costs

[/et_pb_fullwidth_header][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default”]

Recover unpaid electricity costs from telecom operators and protect your property income.

Many landlords are unaware that telecom operators may be connected to their electricity supply, sometimes without proper authorisation or payment. Our expert Electricity Costs Recovery Service helps UK landowners identify unauthorised electricity use, verify energy consumption, and recover unpaid charges efficiently and professionally.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5″ locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”#12557B” custom_padding=”5vh|2vw|5vh|2vw|true|true” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”bd440461-a47f-4b44-986d-3dba84282a26″]

What Is Electricity Costs Recovery?

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” text_orientation=”center” background_layout=”dark” global_colors_info=”{}”]Mobile phone masts and telecom equipment require a continuous power supply to operate. In some cases:

• Operators connect directly to the landlord’s electricity supply without formal rights.
• Sub-meters (check meters) are missing or bypassed, so usage isn’t accurately billed.
• Lease clauses about electricity use and billing are unclear or unenforced.

When electricity costs are under-tracked or unpaid, landlords can lose thousands in avoidable expenses.

Our service helps you identify, verify, and recover those costs, including negotiating retroactive payments where applicable.

 

Why Electricity Costs Matter for Landlords

Telecom sites can consume significant electricity, particularly modern 4G/5G installations. If operators draw power from your supply without proper billing arrangements:

• Your operating costs increase
• Your income from the site decreases
• You may be paying for energy you should be reimbursed for
Without specialist review, this situation can go unnoticed for years.[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5″ global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.23.4″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″]

How Our Electricity Recovery Process Works

We take a structured, end-to-end approach to reclaiming electricity costs:

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_divider _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_divider][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Lease and Supply Review” badge=”1″ blurb_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ custom_css_free_form=”/* Fix Letter Spacing and Bullet Alignment */||.et_pb_text ul {|| list-style-position: outside; /* Ensures bullets don’t ‘hang’ or overlap */|| padding-left: 20px !important; /* Moves the whole list to the left */|| margin-left: 0 !important;||}||||.et_pb_text ul li {|| letter-spacing: 0px !important; /* Reduces space between letters */|| line-height: 1.6em; /* Adjusts vertical spacing for readability */|| margin-bottom: 10px; /* Adds a small gap between each bullet point */|| text-align: left; /* Forces left alignment */||}” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

We begin with a detailed audit of your lease and electricity supply arrangements to identify:

  • Whether the operator has rights to connect to your supply
  • If the lease properly describes electricity billing obligations
  • Where sub-meters should be installed and how utility costs should be allocated

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Metering Verification” badge=”2″ blurb_icon=”h||divi||400″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ min_height=”436.8px” custom_css_free_form=”/* Fix Letter Spacing and Bullet Alignment for Meter Section */||.custom-meter-list {|| list-style-position: outside !important;|| padding-left: 25px !important; /* Matches the previous list for consistency */|| margin-top: 10px !important;|| margin-bottom: 20px !important;||}||||.custom-meter-list li {|| letter-spacing: 0px !important; /* Removes the excess space between letters */|| line-height: 1.6em !important;|| margin-bottom: 10px !important;|| text-align: left !important;|| list-style-type: disc !important;||}” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

We check whether:

  • A sub-meter (check meter) is installed
  • The meter is functioning and accurately recording usage
  • Any bypassing or unmetered connections exist

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Electricity Usage Assessment” badge=”3″ blurb_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ custom_css_free_form=”/* Global Fix for Professional Service Lists */||.custom-audit-list {|| list-style-position: outside !important;|| padding-left: 25px !important; || margin-top: 15px !important;|| margin-bottom: 20px !important;||}||||.custom-audit-list li {|| letter-spacing: 0px !important; /* Tightens the %22stretched%22 letters */|| line-height: 1.6em !important;|| margin-bottom: 12px !important;|| text-align: left !important;|| list-style-type: disc !important;|| color: inherit; /* Ensures it matches your paragraph text color */||}” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

Our team assesses how much electricity has been consumed and whether payments are missing or inadequate. We look for:

  • Unauthorised supply usage
  • Long periods of unbilled energy consumption
  • Billing discrepancies or under-charging

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Negotiation & Recovery” badge=”4″ blurb_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ min_height=”471.6px” custom_css_free_form=”/* Fix Letter Spacing and Bullet alignment for Recovery Section */||.custom-audit-list {|| list-style-position: outside !important;|| padding-left: 25px !important; || margin-top: 15px !important;||}||||.custom-audit-list li {|| letter-spacing: 0px !important; || line-height: 1.6em !important;|| margin-bottom: 12px !important;|| text-align: left !important;|| list-style-type: disc !important;||}” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

We handle negotiations with the operator to recover:

  • Backdated electricity costs
  • Outstanding bills owed under the terms of the lease
  • Equitable compensation for unbilled usage

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.23.4″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Documentation & Resolution Support” badge=”5″ blurb_icon=”||fa||400″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ custom_css_free_form=”/* Fix Letter Spacing for Closing Paragraphs */||.custom-closing-text {|| letter-spacing: 0px !important;|| line-height: 1.6em !important;|| text-align: left !important; /* Prevents justification stretching */|| margin-top: 20px !important;|| font-weight: 500; /* Slightly bolder to emphasize the final service step */||}” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

We prepare all necessary documentation and support dispute resolution if needed to secure your recovery outcome.

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″]

Common Scenarios We Resolve

Why Choose Our Electricity Costs Recovery Service

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_divider _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_divider][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_blurb title=”Unauthorised Power Connections” use_icon=”on” font_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”657f9658-6b13-4e8b-b438-cb36d3830356″ global_colors_info=”{}”]

Operators or tenants drawing electricity without formal rights

[/et_pb_blurb][et_pb_blurb title=”Lease Non-Compliance” use_icon=”on” font_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”657f9658-6b13-4e8b-b438-cb36d3830356″ global_colors_info=”{}”]

Clauses about electricity use ignored or misinterpreted

[/et_pb_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_blurb title=”Missing or Faulty Sub-Meters” use_icon=”on” font_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”657f9658-6b13-4e8b-b438-cb36d3830356″ global_colors_info=”{}”]

No meter, bypassed meter, or inaccurate utility usage data

[/et_pb_blurb][et_pb_blurb title=”Long-term Undetected Use” use_icon=”on” font_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”657f9658-6b13-4e8b-b438-cb36d3830356″ global_colors_info=”{}”]

Years of unrecouped electricity costs found after review

[/et_pb_blurb][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.23.1″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5″ global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.23.1″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.23.1″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _dynamic_attributes=”content” _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″]@ET-DC@eyJkeW5hbWljIjp0cnVlLCJjb250ZW50IjoicG9zdF90aXRsZSIsInNldHRpbmdzIjp7ImJlZm9yZSI6IjxoMj5Db21wcmVoZW5zaXZlICIsImFmdGVyIjoiIE92ZXJ2aWV3PC9oMj4ifX0=@[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default”]

Telecom installations require a reliable power supply, and operators typically have their own direct connection from the Regional Electricity Company (REC) or connect into the landlord’s supply. The lease agreement with the operator should explicitly state if they have the right to connect to the landlord’s supply. Unfortunately, oversights occur, and connections are made without proper authorisation.

Reclaiming unpaid electricity costs for your property can be a complex process that demands expertise and diligence. Our Electricity Costs Recovery service is meticulously designed to address this challenge head-on. From identifying unclaimed electricity or unauthorised connections to negotiating fair compensation, our comprehensive approach ensures that you not only recover what is rightfully yours but also gain peace of mind.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _dynamic_attributes=”content” _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″]@ET-DC@eyJkeW5hbWljIjp0cnVlLCJjb250ZW50IjoicG9zdF90aXRsZSIsInNldHRpbmdzIjp7ImJlZm9yZSI6IjxoMj5XaG8gU2hvdWxkIENvbnNpZGVyIEVsZWN0cmljaXR5IENvc3RzIFJlY292ZXJ5PyIsImFmdGVyIjoiPC9oMj4ifX0=@[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default”]

This service helps:
• Commercial landlords with telecom installations
• Landowners with phone masts or antennae on their property
• Property managers overseeing sites with energy use issues
• Real estate investors with telecom leases

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″ locked=”off”]

Why Specialist Assistance Matters

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” locked=”off”]

Understanding whether an operator should be paying electricity costs, and how much they owe, requires:

• Lease expertise
• Technical verification of metering and supply
• Understanding operator behaviour and typical oversight practices
• Negotiation experience

Without this expertise, landlords can miss substantial recoverable amounts.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″ locked=”off”]

Take Back Control of Your Electricity Costs

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” header_3_text_align=”center” locked=”off”]

Don’t let unauthorised energy usage erode your returns. If you are unsure whether a telecom operator is connected to your electricity supply or if electricity costs haven’t been properly billed, we can help.

Contact us today for a lease audit and start reclaiming what you’re owed.

meta.rank_math_title: Phone Mast Electricity Costs Recovery | Reclaim Unpaid Energy Charges
meta.rank_math_description: Are telecom operators using your power for free? We help UK landlords identify unauthorised connections and recover backdated electricity costs from phone mast sites. Stop energy leaks and secure your property income now.

content.rendered:
[et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ fullwidth=”on” admin_label=”Hero Section” _builder_version=”4.16″ global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_fullwidth_header title=”Lease Retrievals – Recover Your Lost Phone Mast Lease” button_one_text=”Enquire Now” button_one_url=”@ET-DC@eyJkeW5hbWljIjp0cnVlLCJjb250ZW50IjoicG9zdF9saW5rX3VybF9wYWdlIiwic2V0dGluZ3MiOnsicG9zdF9pZCI6IjYxIn19@” _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _dynamic_attributes=”button_one_url” button_one_text_color_hover=”#edbb5f” button_two_text_color_hover=”#edbb5f” button_one_bg_color_hover=”rgba(0,0,0,0)” button_two_bg_color_hover=”rgba(0,0,0,0)”]

Recover Lost Telecom Lease Documents and Reclaim Unpaid Rent (Free Service)

[/et_pb_fullwidth_header][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default”]

Have you lost your Phone Mast lease agreement?

Don’t risk losing valuable rent reviews, backdated rent, or contractual rights because the lease can’t be found. Our free Lease Retrieval Service helps UK landowners recover misplaced or lost Phone Mast lease documents quickly, professionally, and at no cost to you.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5″ locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”#12557B” custom_padding=”5vh|2vw|5vh|2vw|true|true” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”bd440461-a47f-4b44-986d-3dba84282a26″ global_colors_info=”{}”]

What Is Lease Retrieval?

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” text_orientation=”center” background_layout=”dark”]

A lease retrieval is the process of locating, obtaining, and returning a copy of your existing telecom lease when the original document has been lost, misplaced, or never properly recorded. Many landlords don’t realise that without the signed lease in hand, they may be unable to:

• Confirm rent review dates
• Trigger rent increases
• Recover backdated rent
• Verify terms and operator obligations
• Protect redevelopment rights or contract terms

We retrieve the lease on your behalf so you know exactly what rights and obligations apply to your site.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default”]

Why Lease Retrieval Matters

Your lease document is the foundation of your legal and financial rights as a landlord. Without it:

• You may miss crucial rent reviews
• You may not know when terms expire
• Operators can exploit ambiguity
• You could lose income through unclaimed reviews or back rent

Regaining a copy of your lease is the first step toward protecting your interests and maximising the income from your Phone Mast site.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5″ global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″]

How Our Free Lease Retrieval Service Works

We make the process simple and effective – with no upfront cost:

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_divider _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_divider][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Submit Basic Lease Details” badge=”1″ blurb_icon=”||fa||400″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ custom_css_free_form=”/* Intake List Formatting */||.custom-audit-list {|| list-style-position: outside !important;|| padding-left: 25px !important; || margin-top: 10px !important;|| margin-bottom: 20px !important;||}||||.custom-audit-list li {|| letter-spacing: 0px !important; || line-height: 1.6em !important;|| margin-bottom: 10px !important;|| text-align: left !important;|| list-style-type: disc !important;||}” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

Provide us with any information you have about your Phone Mast, for example:

  • Site address
  • Operator’s name
  • Approximate lease date

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Sign a Consent Form” badge=”2″ blurb_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ custom_css_free_form=”/* Fix for Instruction/Process Paragraphs */||.custom-closing-text {|| letter-spacing: 0px !important;|| line-height: 1.7em !important;|| text-align: left !important;|| margin-top: 20px !important;|| font-weight: 400;||}” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

You complete a simple consent form authorising our team to act on your behalf with the mobile operator.

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”We Contact the Operator” badge=”3″ blurb_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ custom_css_free_form=”/* Professional Action Statement Formatting */||.custom-closing-text {|| letter-spacing: 0px !important;|| line-height: 1.7em !important;|| text-align: left !important;|| margin-top: 15px !important;|| margin-bottom: 15px !important;||}” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

We reach out to the telecom operator or their agents and begin the retrieval process, including follow-ups where necessary.

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Lease Delivered to You” badge=”4″ blurb_icon=”||fa||400″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ custom_css_free_form=”/* Final Process & Deliverables Formatting */||.custom-audit-list {|| list-style-position: outside !important;|| padding-left: 25px !important; || margin-top: 10px !important;|| margin-bottom: 15px !important;||}||||.custom-audit-list li {|| letter-spacing: 0px !important;|| line-height: 1.6em !important;|| margin-bottom: 10px !important;|| text-align: left !important;|| list-style-type: disc !important;||}||||.custom-closing-text {|| letter-spacing: 0px !important;|| line-height: 1.7em !important;|| text-align: left !important;|| margin-top: 15px !important;|| font-weight: 500; /* Bold the ‘No Obligation’ promise */||}” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

Once the lease is retrieved, we send it to you and can provide a free review to highlight:

  • Rent review dates
  • Backdated rent opportunities
  • Important terms and clauses

You then decide whether you want further support from us – with no obligation.

meta.rank_math_title: Phone Mast Lease Document Retrieval | UK Landlord Support
meta.rank_math_description: Missing your phone mast lease? We help UK site providers retrieve lost or misplaced lease documents and telecom agreements to ensure your rights and rental income are fully protected.

content.rendered:
[et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ fullwidth=”on” admin_label=”Hero Section” _builder_version=”4.16″ global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_fullwidth_header title=”Phone Mast Removal & Redevelopment – Reclaim Your Land” button_one_text=”Enquire Now” button_one_url=”@ET-DC@eyJkeW5hbWljIjp0cnVlLCJjb250ZW50IjoicG9zdF9saW5rX3VybF9wYWdlIiwic2V0dGluZ3MiOnsicG9zdF9pZCI6IjYxIn19@” _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _dynamic_attributes=”button_one_url” title_font_size_phone=”29px” button_one_text_color_hover=”#edbb5f” button_two_text_color_hover=”#edbb5f” button_one_bg_color_hover=”rgba(0,0,0,0)” button_two_bg_color_hover=”rgba(0,0,0,0)” global_colors_info=”{}”]

Remove or relocate a telecom mast so you can redevelop, repair, or reclaim your property.
Phone masts and telecom equipment sometimes prevent redevelopment, urgent repairs, or full use of your land. We help UK landlords navigate the legal, technical, and negotiation process to remove, relocate, or decommission these installations – safely, effectively, and in compliance with the law.

[/et_pb_fullwidth_header][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″ global_colors_info=”{}”]

What Is Phone Mast Removal & Redevelopment?

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”]

Phone Mast removal and redevelopment is the process of legally disconnecting and removing a telecom installation, such as a mobile mast or rooftop antenna, from your property so you can:

• Carry out redevelopment plans
• Complete essential building or roof repairs
• Eliminate safety risks during construction
• Regain vacant possession of the site
• Ensure your land or building can be used for a new purpose

Telecom equipment can be a barrier to planning permission and site redevelopment, and without professional guidance, the process is complex and legally delicate.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″ global_colors_info=”{}”]

Why You Might Need Removal or Redevelopment Support

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”]

There are several reasons landlords seek mast removal or relocation:

• You plan to redevelop the property but can’t get vacant possession
• Roof or structural repairs are blocked by mast equipment
• The mast impedes new tenancies or business use
• The lease has expired or is unfavourable to you
• The mast affects the property’s value or marketability

Operators do not remove equipment automatically, and in some cases, courts have clarified that redevelopment rights are interpreted narrowly under the law.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5″ global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″ global_colors_info=”{}”]

How Our Mast Removal & Redevelopment Service Works

We guide you through every step, from lease assessment to final site restoration.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_divider _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_divider][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Lease Agreement Review” badge=”1″ blurb_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

We begin with a detailed review of your mast lease to identify legal rights, restrictions, and removal or relocation options.

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Legal Support & ECC Compliance” badge=”2″ blurb_icon=”||divi||400″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

Working with specialist telecoms solicitors, we manage all required legal steps under the Electronic Communications Code (ECC), including notices and documentation necessary for lawful removal.

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Operator Engagement & Negotiation” badge=”3″ blurb_icon=”||divi||400″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

We coordinate communications with the operator, including negotiation of removal terms, relocation options, and any cost-sharing arrangements.

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Project Management” badge=”4″ blurb_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

We help oversee the practical side of removal, including:

• Health & safety coordination
• Timelines and logistics
• Third-party infrastructure management (e.g., power cables or fibre tied to the mast)

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][difl_advanced_blurb title=”Site Restoration” badge=”5″ blurb_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6da1b318-7875-4f8a-8ff3-4093931fca33″ global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22blurb_icon_color%22,%22blurb_icon_color%22%93,%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”]

After removal, we help ensure the property is restored to its pre-installation condition, protecting aesthetics, value, and future use.

[/difl_advanced_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″ global_colors_info=”{}”]

Benefits of Professional Mast Removal Support

Working with specialists gives you:

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_divider _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_divider][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_blurb title=”Legal compliance with all regulatory requirements” use_icon=”on” font_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”657f9658-6b13-4e8b-b438-cb36d3830356″ global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_blurb][et_pb_blurb title=”Reduced risk of delays or disputes” use_icon=”on” font_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”657f9658-6b13-4e8b-b438-cb36d3830356″ global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_blurb][et_pb_blurb title=”Clear planning for redevelopment or repairs ” use_icon=”on” font_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”657f9658-6b13-4e8b-b438-cb36d3830356″ global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_blurb][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_blurb title=”Strategic negotiation with telecom operators” use_icon=”on” font_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”657f9658-6b13-4e8b-b438-cb36d3830356″ global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_blurb][et_pb_blurb title=”Site restoration that protects property value” use_icon=”on” font_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”657f9658-6b13-4e8b-b438-cb36d3830356″ global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_blurb][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”]

Professional guidance is especially important because legal standards for what counts as “redevelopment” under the ECC are evolving, and operators may resist removal without strong legal grounds. 

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″ locked=”off”]

Common Questions About Removal & Redevelopment

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_accordion _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”6b8dd53a-3d47-4a78-9892-ff860c628284″ locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_accordion_item title=”Can I remove a mast before the lease ends?” open=”on” _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”]

Often, yes, especially if you have a solid redevelopment plan or there’s a legal basis under your lease and the ECC. We assess your situation and advise on the best pathway.

[/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=”What if a telecom operator refuses to cooperate?” _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}” open=”off”]

We manage negotiation and, where necessary, legal processes to ensure operator compliance with proper notice procedures and statutory requirements.

[/et_pb_accordion_item][et_pb_accordion_item title=”Who pays for removal and site restoration?” _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}” open=”off”]

This can vary. Some costs may be the landlord’s responsibility, while others can be negotiated with the operator — particularly where redevelopment or lease terms support it.

[/et_pb_accordion_item][/et_pb_accordion][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″ global_colors_info=”{}”]

Ready to Reclaim Your Property?

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”]

Whether you’re planning redevelopment, repairs, or simply want full control of your land, we can help you navigate the Phone Mast removal process with confidence.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_blurb title=”Phone number” use_icon=”on” font_icon=”||fa||900″ _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”213d8aa5-4cb9-4946-a873-4354ecb66e0a” link_option_url=”Tel:+442038830605″ icon_font_size=”24px” use_circle=”on” circle_color=”#edbb5f” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-13f1c606-be05-4b7a-babe-8046e2113f3f%22:%91%22image_icon_background_color%22,%22image_icon_background_color%22%93}”]

The Fort Offices, Artillery Business Park, Oswestry, SY11 4AD

[/et_pb_blurb][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ _builder_version=”4.23.1″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5″ global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-22600b78-b82d-4bae-b782-f3cb617b66f5%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.23.1″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.23.1″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.23.1″ _dynamic_attributes=”content” _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″ global_colors_info=”{}”]@ET-DC@eyJkeW5hbWljIjp0cnVlLCJjb250ZW50IjoicG9zdF90aXRsZSIsInNldHRpbmdzIjp7ImJlZm9yZSI6IjxoMj5Db21wcmVoZW5zaXZlICIsImFmdGVyIjoiIE92ZXJ2aWV3PC9oMj4ifX0=@[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.23.1″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”]

Landlords often require the removal or relocation of a Phone Mast for various compelling reasons:

  • Obtaining planning permission for redevelopment.
  • Necessary relocation or removal of telecommunications equipment for essential roof repairs.
  • Existing Phone Mast equipment negatively impacting the site’s operational use.
  • Carrying out works in proximity to the existing equipment for health and safety reasons, necessitating equipment deactivation during the project.
  • Reclaiming the property for personal use.

You might be wondering how we are able to assist you. The Phone Mast Advice Company Ltd stands as your trusted partner, dedicated to providing professional guidance and tailored solutions for your unique situation.

Lease Agreement Assessment: Central to our service is a meticulous evaluation of your mobile Phone Mast lease agreement. Our team of experts thoroughly reviews the terms and conditions, ensuring you gain a comprehensive understanding of your rights and responsibilities as a landlord. This knowledge serves as your compass for making informed decisions in this dynamic landscape.

Legal rights: Our role involves project management, oversight, and negotiation of terms for the removal and relocation of Phone Mast equipment. The specialised and precise nature of Phone Mast law necessitates collaboration with a specialist telecoms solicitor to ensure all legalities are correctly addressed. We partner with a select group of specialist solicitors to secure the best results. We agree on the terms, and the solicitors ensure that all legal notices are served accurately and promptly, with proper completion of the legal documentation. It is essential that operators perceive your compliance with proper channels and adherence to the correct procedures to take your requests seriously.

Mast Removal Advisory: Removing a mobile Phone Mast is a complex undertaking, requiring specialised knowledge and precision. The intricacies of dismantling such equipment should not be underestimated. We offer the guidance and expertise required to navigate this significant step seamlessly. Our role is to ensure your interests are safeguarded, your property is preserved, and the process adheres to all legal and technical requirements.

Seamless Site Restoration: Following mast removal, our focus shifts to restoring your property to its original condition. Our dedicated team ensures that any alterations made to accommodate the mast are reversed, preserving both the aesthetics and the value of your land.

Third-Party Considerations: We acknowledge that complexities may arise from third-party equipment, such as power cables. Rest assured, we address these intricacies, providing you with the peace of mind that your property and interests are protected at every juncture.

At The Phone Mast Advice Company Ltd, we are more than advisors; we are your partners in the mast removal process. With decades of experience underpinning our team, we equip you with the finest strategies, legal insights, and technical know-how, ensuring that your property’s future is entrusted to capable hands. Together, we can unlock the full potential of your property. Contact us for a free consultation.

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″ locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”]

Example Scenario

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.5″ _module_preset=”default” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”]

A commercial landlord in Birmingham was unable to proceed with a multi-storey redevelopment due to a rooftop telecom mast. Our team coordinated legal termination under the ECC, managed all operator negotiations, and restored site access—clearing the way for planning approval within five months.

[/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ disabled_on=”on|on|on” module_id=”form” _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”gcid-a5e01c3a-717f-40df-b5f2-b1ba08b21a19″ disabled=”on” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-a5e01c3a-717f-40df-b5f2-b1ba08b21a19%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”][et_pb_row _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” background_color=”gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9″ custom_padding=”2em|2em|2em|2em|true|true” box_shadow_style=”preset1″ box_shadow_color=”rgba(0,0,0,0.1)” global_colors_info=”{%22gcid-82bc3aa3-97e9-4b68-ad42-b9f7d8e356e9%22:%91%22background_color%22%93}”][et_pb_column type=”4_4″ _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”191a735f-4790-4aa5-b3a6-6e9fea02f062″]

Get In Touch

[/et_pb_text][et_pb_divider _builder_version=”4.22.2″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_divider][et_pb_code _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”]
